steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Translating a graph 6 units down means subtracting 6 from every $y$-value. In $y = mx + b$, a vertical shift changes only the constant term, not the coefficient of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 7x + 6$: adds 6, which shifts the graph up 6 units. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 7x - 6$: subtracts 6 from the constant term — a vertical shift down 6 units. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = 7(x + 6)$: rewrites as $y = 7x + 42$, which creates a horizontal shift to the left and a large vertical offset. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 7(x - 6)$: rewrites as $y = 7x - 42$, a horizontal shift right. ✗
B is correct.
